title: SubstrateFrontendAuditCompleted payload
description: Emitted by the chain agent-substrate-frontend F5 retrospective to record that the dashboard surfaces serving the event ledger (per-entity timelines, audit-ledger page, dispatch-policy peek) have been verified against the chain's design doc and completion criteria. Self-hosting check parallel to ArchitectureAuditCompleted — the frontend exercising the substrate to record its own audit outcome. Findings are keyed to the design doc's sections (docs/SUBSTRATE_FRONTEND.md §1–§12), each entry recording whether the section's contract is honored end-to-end. See docs/SUBSTRATE_FRONTEND.md and the chain's completion_condition for context.

audit_doc.description: Pointer to the design / contract doc the findings are keyed against — typically docs/SUBSTRATE_FRONTEND.md plus the chain's completion criteria.

summary.description: One- or two-paragraph executive summary of the audit outcome covering all completion-criterion sub-items (a)–(f).

recommended_next_phase.description: Free-text recommendation for the follow-on work (typically Phase 4 — legacy free-form field deprecation and a query-telemetry-substrate completion to wire the related_queries surface end-to-end). Optional.

findings.description: One entry per design-doc section or completion-criterion item. status uses the same enum as the architecture audit (pass / fail / partial / deferred).

item.description: Design-doc section identifier and short description (e.g. "§3 cursor pagination on event_id").

status.description: pass = contract honored end-to-end; fail = gap (must file follow-on); partial = honored with documented gap; deferred = explicitly out of scope per chain non-goals.

evidence.description: Concrete evidence: test names, file paths, screenshots, commit SHA, etc.

severity.description: Severity if status is fail/partial. n/a for informational rows.
